UNITED STATES SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION
Washington, D.C. 20549

SCHEDULE 14A
PROXY STATEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 14(A) OF THE
SECURITIES EXCHANGE ACT OF 1934

Filed by: Keystone Logistics Inc.
Annual Meeting Date: June 8, 2023

EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION

SUMMARY COMPENSATION TABLE

Name and Principal Position | Total Compensation
Chief Executive Officer     | $9,100,000
Chief Financial Officer     | $3,400,000

CEO Pay Ratio: The ratio of the annual total compensation of our CEO to the median annual total compensation of all employees was 87 to 1. The median annual total compensation of all employees was $104,598.

ADVISORY VOTE ON EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION (SAY-ON-PAY)

Proposal 1: Advisory Vote to Approve Named Executive Officer Compensation

The Board of Directors recommends a vote FOR this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      62,450,000   (88.2%)
Votes Against:   7,800,000   (11.0%)
Abstentions:       550,000    (0.8%)
Broker Non-Votes: 3,200,000

The proposal received the support of 88.2% of votes cast.

SHAREHOLDER PROPOSALS

Proposal 3: Shareholder Proposal Regarding Supply Chain Labor Standards

Submitted by: Trillium Asset Management

RESOLVED, that shareholders of Keystone Logistics Inc. request that the Board of Directors adopt a comprehensive supply chain labor standards policy requiring all tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers to certify compliance with ILO core labor conventions, including prohibitions on forced labor, child labor, and the right to organize, with annual third-party audits of high-risk suppliers.

The Board of Directors recommends a vote AGAINST this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      31,200,000   (44.1%)
Votes Against:  38,500,000   (54.4%)
Abstentions:     1,100,000    (1.6%)

Proposal 4: Shareholder Proposal Regarding Greenhouse Gas Emissions Targets

Submitted by: Boston Common Asset Management

RESOLVED, that shareholders request the Board establish science-based greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets consistent with the Paris Agreement goal of limiting warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius, covering all Scopes 1, 2, and 3 emissions.

The Board of Directors recommends a vote AGAINST this proposal.

VOTE RESULTS:
Votes For:      19,800,000   (28.0%)
Votes Against:  49,600,000   (70.1%)
Abstentions:     1,400,000    (2.0%)
